Program plate is W871706 and the locking plate is W871708. Went ahead and changed this on the white board. Double checked with the parts spreadsheet.
If we start getting piston faults, we are going to try the other program and locking plates and see if that helps.
I did see that, I'm saying the program/locking plates that we want to try first were noted incorrectly on the white board.

We are requesting new barrels from SV so we can flush out the dark chocolate on the line. PTL is currently at 74% full and buffer tank is at 40% full. Team will focus on washing HER molds, load bequet molds, assemble mano depositor. FYI Alan Todd
Chocolate adjustment report Alan Todd Alex Mendoza
Chocolate adjustment report Alan Todd Brandon Lance
Bequet changeover on FW is complete.

New barrels have arrived & are being staged now. 

Alex Mendoza - Luis Pino
Line closure adjustment. Brandon Lance Alan Todd Nidia Lopez Corea Sofia Carcamo
Sending chocolate from PTL to buffer tank. We will start to empty out the dark chocolate in buffer tank from the branch pump. Just waiting for QA to swab barrels so we can start. Alan Todd
Okay thank you

We have the tarp in this area sealed now. 
Curt Willis Dakota Adair
Checked EPJs throughout shift and did not find any instance where cardboard was jammed into battery unit.
Dakota Adair Conner Boyd. Please have someone seal the bottom of this curtain
All matrixes are clean
Check all the EPJs and didn't find any cardboard jammed in the battery
William  Lopez we have a loose ceiling tile by the front restrooms. Can you please take a look at it?
Jody Howse please follow up with your EE's on leaving dishwashing area clean for the next shift.

Hi Jody Howse. It’s true that we no longer have the flavorings for the truffle. Only flammable products should be stored there. I will speak with the operators to make sure they only place flammable products in that area. However, we still have the vanilla and butter sprays. I think we may still need them. Thank you for your question.

Marcos we don't need the old Sollich 1 kitchen flammable cabinet right?  It was for the truffle flavorings but is currently being used as a shelf for lecithin








Does it need to move to L3?
All EPJ's have been inspected for cardboard and wood in batteries. Nothing was found. Justin Garner Curt Willis

The drain pipe for the belt washer is not supposed to be inside the drain
I will remove it

Shift exchange, changeovers section was updated, to request current caramel being run, and next one to be run. Also gray out chocolate for S4 as we do not use chocolate on that line.
Curt Willis Justin Garner Dakota Adair Jody Howse
We are still experiencing issues at  Cavanna, maintenance continues to work on it.
Maintenance fixed it
Brake system on the Cavanna is now back in spec and the film is running much smoother
Imperial belt, metal detector belt and C6 belt are running correctly
Cavanna technician is here

Cavanna tech is here?

Who is that Robert Villarreal?
He is Scott, the technician at cavannas.
An adjustment is made to the mixing water at 12:43.

The supersack setup has been completed and we are already using it. Ben came to inspect it and asked us to call him once the supersack is almost full so he can verify everything is working properly from a safety standpoint.

Check weight update


Weight check update

Rose SmuinPreston Elmer,

2) we can do some cleaning on the utility side of the Aasted (I will send an email shortly)
Imperial belt, metal detector belt and C6 belt are running correctly
Maintenance are working on Cavana because some good pieces keep falling down.
Sensor for auger door isn’t registering it’s closed
Maintenance adjusted where the jaws sat, they were too low which was causing good chocolate pieces to drop down after being cut
Bracket of the sensor on the door was bent causing it not to read correctly
Capture test is within acceptable range.
Capture test
SuperSack has the label with the correct lot and BB date. All in good standards
Line is performing well. Product meets specifications. Seal integrity is in good condition. Weight and appearance are acceptable. No issues to report.
We have problem with Cavanna the product coming out open and double,  maintenance is working on it.
This was found in the allergen cabinet. Grey Boscan and Raoul Lubain please remind your teams not to use non allergen tools for allergen products, I reminded my team already.
Imperial belt, metal detector belt and C6 belt are running correctly
Maintenance is making an adjustment on the jaws.

Scott is working at Cavanna.

Cavanna is running better, but the flow wrap is still opening at times. Scott continues making adjustments.
Demolding, metal detector and C6 belts are running correctly

Scott installed temporary guides, this has helped with the infeed of the cavanna.
 
Scott continues to work on the line.
We start loading molds.

Preston Elmer, Matt Hatch

Do we have anymore of this wire to make a loop at the top of the other ingredient holding tank?

Tg 3 

TG5

TG4

There is no printer
Ok it's burnin. Put the date you want on it.
I talked to TG they will be back in an hour after the bars start coming out.
We are getting ready to fill the depositor but there is still chocolate coming out
We are having the same issue with depositor 4. There are still chocolate streaks. We are pushing white chocolate into a barrel.
Getting a lot of chocolate
Calling QA for a check
QA is passing it off 🎉
Chocolate has passed off
Depositor 4 has passed off as well
10 minutes we will deposit 300 molds
We have leaky nozzles on the left side of depositor 1

Teams, this is VERY important and cannot happen again. I hope the grid survived this instance.
 
The 2380 printers on ML4 Packout cannot be cleaned with ANYTHING other than the cleaning kits. It is a maintenance task for now and cannot be touched by anything other than the items in that cleaning kit and the cleaning solution provided in that kit. It uses mineral oil for cleaning and sanitary wipes WILL ruin these $24,000 printers.  Itzel Chavez Tyson Fausett Kimberly Morales Nathan Gevenois Grey Boscan Frederick Peairs Raoul Lubain Rich Brierley Marcos Montano Alan Greer Rose Smuin
 
 
Here you can see part of it still wet from sani-wipes, this was today

 
Everyone
Cleaning the slider didn’t work we are pulling out the depositor.
Good try and thank you for the update.
We have taken it out and started to take it apart to see if there is an issue we can’t see
Has anyone reached out to WDS on the issue?

We have the pictures of the continuous leaking on the west side. 

Can you take pictures as we take it apart and put it back together Tyson Fausett?
Nozzle plate passed ATP
Sliders passed ATP
Not yet, I was waiting to see if we could figure it out. I will send an email if we don't figure it out and share all the findings.
I’ll see what I can get
Screen and mixer passed ATP
Hopper passed ATP
So far the operators haven’t found anything out of the ordinary. We should get it back in before the end of the shift.
Are there gaskets that go in the channel on the picture above?
No. There is gaskets in between the nozzle plate and the heating block attached to it and those were checked during cleaning and they were fine
Dry test went okay. But as soon as we used chocolate we got another alert.
Can someone reach in and get a video behind here while it's trying to dose? To see the slider couplers while moving.
We found different plates. But we are still getting cavitation and faulting. This plate doesn’t use the top row of the pistons.
Do we know which program plates are supposed to go on or is it a guessing game?
I have tried both we have for these pistons and they both did the same thing. But the sheet I have doesnt list the program plates
Chad Stone Rose Smuin Preston Elmer  at this time we can't move forward with Depositor #1. I can leave operators with maintenance to troubleshoot , start MFM and send EEs home
The first set using 120 pistons is correct according to quote and change parts list.
Handle broke after being bumped off cart when washing it.
We have sent all extra EE's home, we kept 4 ML4 operators to do some cleaning and rotate lunches

Good morning Frederick Peairs,

Appreciate the teams managing the situation. 

We have the call with WDS in 10 min so please ensure you and Itzel are on it.
Waiting on hopper temperature to be on target and we will start depositing the 300 molds of plain bars.
300 molds are now deposited. We are getting ready to do the capture test.

Lot and date is correct and It's readable



Caddies
The temperature of the south side is in the range
Mold jam after depositor 4.
Preston Elmer is helping operators fix the mold jam with the gameboy.
TG4 will not be operational; it lacks the printer required for this flow wrapper, and we are currently awaiting its arrival.
Maintenance is working on TG5 because it is cutting the flow wrap.

Preston working on the heating unit for the inclusion system. 
 
I will update when we have more information.
Depositor 1 checklist
Depositor 4 checklist
Ptls checklist
The 300 molds are coming out from demolding, we are testing TG 3 and 5
Do the hoses have to be hooked up a certain way?
I saw them hooking them up but I couldn't tell if they were very well marked.

Chad Stone 
Preston is on the phone with WDS
 
Hoses are installed correct.
Preston just added water, they are still troubleshooting

The inclusion hopper was not turned on.


 


Itzel will create a SU checklist for the inclusion hopper
Juan is doing the capture test now.
Recipe has been changed for 60g CNC.
Inclusion was missing a guide, and the other one it’s loose. We are placing the one missing and tightening the other one.

The team was trying to lower the top (raise the hopper) and were going the wrong way. 

In doing so they snapped the bearing frame. The process still works and we will need to order a new one. 



To lower the top (raise the hopper up) turn to the left. 



Itzel Chavez will add stickers today.
We are ready to start, currently filling up hopper on depositor #1 to start depositing.
Everyone Please refresh safetychain to get the latest scrap form update.

Capture test 






The cookie percentage is in the range

Chiara was on the conference call for a total of 4 hours 😲 and Alex only made it to 3 😂. She had to leave but she said she will be available by Teams message if we need recipe support by 10-10:30. Itzel Chavez Rose Smuin Rich Brierley Chad Stone
Capture test completed, and within the range.
We are depositing.
The pallets built by the third shift were assembled incorrectly; we are going to fix them.
Five pallets from the night shift were assembled incorrectly; we are proceeding to organize them correctly.
The line is rejecting many pouches due to high weight—despite containing the correct number of pieces, QA has approved fix  the maximum weight limit to 211 g.
We have opened two pallets, and both pallets contain both horizontal and vertical boxes, with labels affixed to both sides of the boxes.
The 5 pallets were organized correctly.

We are having problems with the machine; it turns off by itself due to not having the perfect combination. However, the machine is full of product and there are no open buckets. Maintenance reported that the 3rd shift had the same problem, and the solution was to reduce the speed to 38 PPM. However, we have tried this and it does not work; the machine still will not turn on. Maintenance is on the line.
The previous problem in the matrix was resolved, but now the machine is running very slowly; we have a speed of 43 PPM, however, 37 PPM are coming out, maintenance continues on the line
The line stopped, it went off again, maintenance is still on the line
Trays and bins were placed in the South matrices’ X-ray area to prevent fallen bags from touching the floor and being returned to the conveyor belt.

The metal detector went off more than three times in a row!! I spoke with the technicians, and they're already checking to see what the problem is!! I swiped the cards, and they were accepted without any issues.
Thank you
Belt C1 stopped for a few minutes. Maintenance personnel are currently on-site investigating the issue, as this same belt stopped twice during the previous shift.
We raised the sensitivity from 700 to 800. If it keeps rejecting without an obvious cause, we should clean the belt
Belt C1 has stopped again; maintenance is on the way.
Shift 3
C1 stopped again mainatances is working on it
Belt is running again
C1 scraper assembly found first thing this morning not assembled issue has been resolved
This won't have air for about one hour while I add a ball valve to the air system. I am leaving the ladder so we can turn it back on when I finish.
We also tied some different air line together to better route the flow to Line 2 and allow construction on line 1. It shouldn't affect production but please let me know if there are any issues.
Thank you for posting.  Can we make an adjustment to bring the chocolate down to 3.4 please.
The higher-than-normal number of joined pieces (doubles, triples, etc.) were due to different employees adding marshmallows during lunch rotations.
I am seeing an improvement, thanks for the help

Given the problems we've had with the silver line on the pouches in MM3 and even more so in MM4, very few in MM5 because it works very slowly. 











These lines appear mostly on the back of the pouch, but we also see them on the front in some cases. Thicknesses range from 0.58mm to 1.96mm. 


We've asked production to collect data to find out how frequently this defect occurs, and we have an update.





- MM3: 7 pallets /240 = 1680 pouches


134 pouches With a silver line 


 


MM4: 6 pallets /240 = 1440 pouches


184 pouches with a silver line 


These pouches are being approved, we hope they can improve this flaw. Production must continue collecting data and QA will take measurements of the mm. Charity Young Miriah Manzanares

Thank you for collecting this data. It’s so helpful for us to supply operations informed data to determine next steps. 
We will collect visual samples of 1.5mm, 2mm, 3mm to dial in more of “go-no-go”.

As agreed, we will not place any thing on hold, and will develop AQL’s as soon as we get buy in from other stakeholders. 

Great teamwork William Victor Conner Angelica
We are here to work as a team and improve everything we can
Caramel cook times
The team is working on that already
Depositor EoS
And also taking care of things like this, making sure we don't have product stuck in the trays
Enrober EoS
And EE was sticking the labels in the wrong way, the rest of the EE are reworking the pallets looking like that and I am making sure is according to the spec William Beattie Angelica Perez

~31 and ~32 minute cook times on S3
Curt Willis

We looked over MM4 and found no issues in the bag arms. However, we did find that there is flex in the hot seal plates when they close. It is very small, but would be enough to effect the bags and create the silver line. I have put in a parts request for kits to rebuild the hot seal assembly.

234 bags of scrap of 07: am a 09: am.  Many bags were bursting on Matrix 4 due to the bag's silver coating; the machine is currently down, awaiting a solution from the mechanics.
MM3 pallet 1-13 13 pouches between all those pallets that had silver lining.  MM5 5 pallets had 12 pouches between all the pallets with silver lining.  Justin and Katya
Weight checks - 7:00am to 9:30am.
Operator had noticed the gripper pads we worn out.  We have informed maintenance about this and they are replacing them now
Great job on chocolate usage, Jesus

Ok MM4 is running good.  Adjustments we have done is change out all grippers for the pouch grabbers, timing for sealer bar from 1.2 to 1.7, fill station was on same station fill and is now on previous station as per CL.    Guide transitioning railing for the pouchs was to far out and was brought in to help with pouches not swinging.  Hopper vibration was off we had turned it on.  More tension was put onto the pouches to help with making them square.
After maintenance changed the pads, the pouch improved. Very few have silver, less than 2 mm.

I am performing the pouch check on M3, and the production time was showing 05:42 and was not updating. I spoke with the supervisor, and he made the adjustment just now, and it is now showing the real production time. I would like to know if the M3 production should be placed on hold
Miriah Manzanares, Mary Montilla, Charity Young
message received, will review impact
Lot verified
The Pouches looks good ✅

The line is running

Hello! Just a heads up, the supply count has been completed & we need the following ordered asap. 

Machine box tape (S3 only version) & new pallets. (40776) 

Junior Quinonez-Curt Willis, Sunny Jagtiani-Ethan Bean-Tiffanee Taylor-Martin Carlos

Yes.
Good morning, Candon Watt, Sunny Jagtiani, Martin Carlos, Ethan Bean . For FAD MK on Friday, the BOM requires chocolate 10121, which we don't have. Is 10200 compatible?
Also, for Monday, the BOM for S3 Pretzel thins requires 10109, which we don't have. Can we use 10257 or 10227 instead?

Jazmin Vazquez 10200 is compatible, but I would rather you use 10226 that is also compatible, and is where the bulk of our inventory is.
Candon Watt to approve.
Thanks
Jazmin Vazquez Yes, you could use either 10257 or 10227. We have plenty of both.
Awesome, thank you! We will go based on FIFO for this one. Francisco Gudino Torres Ricardo Tovar
That’s correct

Curt Willis & Daniel Bringas, 
 
Marcos Jimenez recommends that tomorrow we make the XR80 DE62 SS caramel instead of the Classic, since it will be Volker's (Chocotech Technician) last day onsite and he can help us create the recipe.  We haven't made that caramel on this line yet.
 
Please let me know if that works so we can have Jazmin Vazquez request the materials for it.
That works for me 👍

We have 4681 (pieces)/Wax rolls in Springville. Did we want Francisco to order these over?
Curt Willis Dakota Adair

Curt Willis, Justin Garner, Dakota Adair, & Chris Givens, 
 
Tomorrow we will focus on creating the Kitchen 4 and Twist Wrap recipes for the XR80 caramel. This will also be Volker’s (Chocotech Technician) last day onsite. 2nd shift will come in early for training, and I recommend we target changeover training for 2nd shift Maintenance around 5 PM.
 
Thursday, we can either continue cooking XR80 or switch back to Classic caramel. This will be Cornell & Mathias’s (Theegarten Technicians) last day onsite. I recommend scheduling changeover training for 1st shift Maintenance around 7:30 AM, then continuing operator training throughout the rest of the day.
Curt Willis first batch of caramel XR80 is on the reserve tank in case you can to come and check it out
Thank you
Luis Pino, do we have the Bequet molds onsite?

Joey Michael Dudney, Jovani Castellanos, Junior Quinonez, Ethan Bean, Ira Robison
 
We are supposed to receive item 41219 Bequet Cup Flow Wrap tomorrow via FedEx. Please update here once the order arrives. Ethan Bean has the tracking info and can help post on when it should arrive and if there are any delays.
 
Chad Stone, Alex Mendoza, Brandon Lance, Gustavo Rodas, Luis Pino - FYI
We have two pallets on site. Luis sent a request to get the rest from SV.

Ethan Bean do you have an ETA on Bequet FW? Maintenance is working on the changeover and would like to use the actually FW we will be running. Right now they are using a roll of favorite day truffles FW just to test.
Update: Cashews for Line Sorters Test have a 1:00 pm ETA into Lindon 2

Hi team, I’m sending the order we should follow for Brookie Grinding based on what we physically have available and the expiration dates. Alan Todd Mathew Draper Brandon Lance Gustavo Rodas FYI Wilson Amaris Cabrera Claudio Tovar
This is great!! Thank you Sofia Carcamo
Jovani Castellanos can you please pull the brownies from the oldest date? It looks like we have four pallets at the 20th.
I’m on it
Eric Ortega, Raymond Fleming can you guys check on the grinder? It’s not turning on.
Ok
Urshel is running now
What was the issue Mathew Draper, Eric Ortega?
We need a special key for the locks on the safety side and it wasn't letting it run because it wasn't on the lock position
This lock was in the unlocked position. Maintenance was able to turn it with some vice grips to the locked position and it started running.
Good morning, this WO will be used in item 21081 for BB 2/21/2027
Midshift 1st shift
Warehouse has requested that we no longer use the form on the left and only use the form on the right to request materials.
Mid shift numbers

We just did a changeover and We are getting a “piston servo fault”. 
Everyone any ideas that might help cause this. The pistons aren’t going in to the slider
Tyson Fausett, what is your torque set at?
We can go up to 200 right if needed?

Pistons are 80%
sliders are 120%
We can try that, it would have to be maintenance that changes it we don’t have access to
Tyson Fausett, are you trying to purge the hopper?
Can you switch recipes and check for torque for depositor 2 caramel on filled bars? 200 is max, I would try 100 first. I don't want to just turn up the torque, if that is not the issue.
Yes. That’s what we were starting to do but when we put chocolate into the hopper, the chocolate was coming out of the nozzles on the left side.
Did you get a video you can share so I can see what is happening?
Sorry I didn’t. The left side wasn’t going in as far as the right side then would fault out.
The chocolate was just pouring out.
Thinking about it more Tyson Fausett I wonder if the pistons were never going in far enough and that allowed for the chocolate to leak out?.?.
Tyson Fausett, check to see if the rotary slider is even in place.
That’s definitely possible from the way it was acting

I would also check your rotary slider is in the correct position or the gasket on the nozzle plate was put in correctly. Those two things can cause leaks if they aren’t set correctly. Also make sure you have the correct program plates and that they are installed correctly if you are getting piston faults.

Sliders were the first thing we checked. Everything is matching and in the correct position as far as we know. 
We are taking it apart and cleaning it
The higher-than-normal number of joined pieces (doubles, triples, etc.) were due to different employees adding marshmallows during lunch rotations.
I am seeing an improvement, thanks for the help
